thanos.name: Thanos
thanos.short_description: Open source system that extends Prometheus with a global query view and unlimited metrics retention via object storage.

features.description: Query metrics across multiple Prometheus servers and clusters from a single endpoint.
features.title: Global Query View

features.description: Store historical metrics indefinitely by offloading data to object storage such as S3, GCS, Azure, Swift, or Tencent COS.
features.title: Unlimited Retention

features.description: Works with Grafana and any other tool that supports the Prometheus Query API.
features.title: Prometheus API Compatible

features.description: Optimizes long-range historical queries and lets teams configure data retention policies.
features.title: Downsampling & Compaction

pros: Free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
CNCF incubating project with an active community and Slack
Works natively with existing Grafana and Prometheus tooling
Enables effectively unlimited metrics retention via object storage
Used in production by large organizations including Adobe, ByteDance, eBay, and Monzo
cons: Self-hosted only; no managed cloud offering
Requires configuring and operating an object storage backend
Adds operational complexity on top of running Prometheus itself

faqs.answer: Yes. Thanos is open source under the Apache 2.0 license with no paid tier.
faqs.question: Is Thanos free to use?

faqs.answer: No. Thanos extends Prometheus by adding global querying across clusters and long-term storage in object storage.
faqs.question: Does Thanos replace Prometheus?

faqs.answer: Yes. Thanos exposes a Prometheus-compatible query API that Grafana and other Prometheus-aware tools can use.
faqs.question: Does Thanos work with Grafana?

faqs.answer: Yes, Thanos is a Cloud Native Computing Foundation incubating project.
faqs.question: Is Thanos a CNCF project?
